packet_markdown:
# AVA Hollywood at La Pietra Place building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: ava-hollywood-la-pietra-6677-w-santa-monica-blvd
- Address: 6677 W Santa Monica Blvd
- Primary URL: https://www.avaloncommunities.com/california/los-angeles-apartments/ava-hollywood-at-la-pietra-place/
- Management company: AvalonBay Communities
- Recommended status: rejected
- Last updated: 2026-03-22

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 1 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Management: 3 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 2)
- Community stability: 3 (low, evidence count 1)
- Location fit: 4
- Pricing transparency: 4

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: high
- Location noise risk: medium
- Security risk: low
- Pricing risk: low

## Evidence summary
- [official] The official Avalon page still markets a large amenity package, including chill spaces with Wi-Fi, a 5,000-square-foot fitness center, and a pool. Source: AvalonBay official site (https://www.avaloncommunities.com/california/los-angeles-apartments/ava-hollywood-at-la-pietra-place/). Note: Official site highlights lounge seating with Wi-Fi, a 5,000 sq. ft. fitness center, and pool access.
- [review] Current third-party listings still show large active inventory with clear published pricing and floor-plan ranges. Source: Zillow (https://www.zillow.com/apartments/los-angeles-ca/ava-hollywood-at-la-pietra-place/CjjCbw/). Note: Zillow showed studio to larger homes with in-unit washer/dryer and current pricing on March 22, 2026.
- [review] Apartment Finder still showed 16 ratings and reviews, so public resident signal exists even though the repo keeps a quiet-related hard stop. Source: Apartment Finder (https://www.apartmentfinder.com/California/Los-Angeles-Apartments/Ava-Hollywood-At-La-Pietra-Place-Apartments-rqlz090). Note: Apartment Finder listed 16 ratings and reviews in the current public profile.

## Open questions
- None beyond normal unit-level verification.

## Notes
Rejected because the repo’s older structural sound-transfer note is still decision-relevant and has not been affirmatively disproved by current public evidence. Current leasing pages look healthy, but they do not erase a previously confirmed floor/ceiling defect for a quiet-first search.
